title: Infosys clarifies DGGI GST refund show cause notice of Rs 414.88 Crores
detailed_summary: Infosys clarified media reports dated 8 th  October 2025 regarding a Directorate General of GST Intelligence show cause notice related to alleged ineligible ITC refunds. DGGI issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties, alleging that services provided by Infosys's overseas branches are not export of services and that refunds claimed were erroneous. Infosys said it assessed the notice, sought external tax and legal advice, and filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka on 19 th  September 2025 challenging the legitimacy of the notice. Infosys stated there is no tax demand as on date and no material impact of the article on the Company.

key_facts.fact: Media reports published on 8 th  October 2025 referred to a show cause notice issued by Directorate General of GST Intelligence for Rs 415 crore to Infosys over alleged ineligible ITC refunds.
DGGI had in May 2025 sought information on the GST refunds claimed by the Company, and Infosys provided relevant information and held meetings with DGGI officials.
DGGI issued a pre-show cause on 30 th  July 2025, after which Infosys sought additional information/relevant documents and time to file a response.
DGGI issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties.
The show cause notice alleged that services provided by Infosys's overseas branches are not export of services and hence the refund claimed by the Company is allegedly erroneous.
Infosys filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka on 19 th  September 2025 challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice.
Infosys stated there is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.

litigation_or_regulatory_matters.description: DGGI issued a show cause notice alleging that services provided by Infosys's overseas branches are not export of services and that GST refunds claimed by the Company are allegedly erroneous.
litigation_or_regulatory_matters.amount: Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties
litigation_or_regulatory_matters.status: The Company has on 19 th  September 2025, filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice. There is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.

risk_factors.risk: DGGI show cause notice alleges ineligible ITC refunds related to services provided by Infosys's overseas branches.
risk_factors.potential_impact: Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties

important_text_spans.text: DGGI did not provide the additional time requested and instead issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the amount and timing of the show cause notice.

important_text_spans.text: As per the show cause notice from DGGI, the services provided by the company's overseas branches are not export of services and hence the refund claimed by the Company is allegedly erroneous.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the nature of the GST refund allegation.

important_text_spans.text: Pursuant to the advice, the Company has on 19 th  September 2025, filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports Infosys's legal response to the notice.

important_text_spans.text: There is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the stated current status of the matter.
